question: Based on the scatter plot of daily screen time vs. sleep, which conclusion is most supported by the data?

options.A: There is a generally negative relationship between screen time and sleep.
options.B: As daily screen time increases, reported sleep tends to increase overall.
options.C: People with the largest and darkest points get much more sleep than others, indicating a dramatically stronger effect.
options.D: There is no relationship at all between screen time and sleep.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the x-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
